latest.title: Total Voting Rights
latest.content:
Today 15:57
RNS Number : 3057G
Synthomer PLC
29 May 2026
29 May 2026
Synthomer plc
(the "Company")
Total voting rights
The following information is disclosed in accordance with DTR 5.6.1R of the Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules of the Financial Conduct Authority (the "
FCA
").
As at the date of this announcement, the Company's issued ordinary share capital consists of 163,997,629 ordinary shares of £0.01 each (the "
Ordinary Shares
"), each carrying the right to one vote at a general meeting of the Company. The Company does not hold any Ordinary Shares in treasury.
Therefore, the total number of voting rights attributable to the Ordinary Shares is 163,997,629.
The figure in the preceding sentence may be used by shareholders as the denominator for the calculations by which they will determine if they are required to notify their interest in, or a change to their interest in, the Ordinary Shares under the FCA's Disclosure Guidance and Transparency Rules.
Enquiries:
Faisal Tabbah, Vice President Investor Relations
Tel: +44 (0) 7813 210 809

latest.title: Notice of AGM
latest.content:
Today 17:21
RNS Number : 5340F
Synthomer PLC
22 May 2026
Synthomer plc
Notice of Annual General Meeting 2026
Synthomer plc (the "Company") announces that it has today published the notice of its 2026 annual general meeting ("AGM") on its website at
www.synthomer.com
.
The Company's AGM will be held at 10.00 am on 22 June 2026.
In accordance with UK Listing Rule 6.4.1, copies of the following documents are being submitted to the Financial Conduct Authority and will shortly be available for inspection via the National Storage Mechanism at:
https://data.fca.org.uk/#/nsm/nationalstoragemechanism
:
·
Notice of 2026 annual general meeting; and
·
Form of proxy.
Copies of the notice of AGM and the 2025 Annual Report and Accounts are also available on the Company's website or on request from the Company Secretary, Synthomer plc, 10 Greycoat Place, London SW1P 1SB.
Legal Entity Identifier (LEI): 213800EHT3TI1KPQQJ56.

latest.title: Application for Admission to Trading
latest.content:
Today 13:51
RNS Number : 4730D
Synthomer PLC
07 May 2026
Synthomer plc(the "Company")
Application for Admission of Further Securities to Trading
Further to the Company's announcement on 30 April 2026 of its final results for the year ended 31 December 2025 and the refinancing of its key debt facilities, the Company has allotted and issued 430,008 new ordinary shares of £0.01 each in the capital of the Company (representing approximately 0.26% of the Company's enlarged share capital following such issuance) (the "
New Ordinary Shares
") to certain of the Group's lenders in satisfaction of certain of the fees payable to them in connection with the refinancing.
An application has been made to London Stock Exchange plc ("
LSE
") for the New Ordinary Shares to be admitted to trading on the LSE's main market for listed securities ("
Admission
"). It is expected that Admission will become effective on or before 8.00 a.m. on 8 May 2026.
This information is provided by RNS, the news service of the London Stock Exchange. RNS is approved by the Financial Conduct Authority to act as a Primary Information Provider in the United Kingdom. company_30s.what_they_do: SYNT is a specialty chemical manufacturer that produces polymer resins used in automotive parts, packaging, and industrial coatings. Their products are engineered for high performance and low environmental impact.
company_30s.why_they_matter: The global polymer market is expanding as manufacturers seek lighter, more durable materials. SYNT’s niche resins give it a competitive edge in sectors that demand high‑quality, low‑toxicity solutions, positioning it as a potential partner for major OEMs and packaging firms.
company_30s.current_state: SYNT has been navigating a period of financial strain, but recent board reshuffles and a fresh refinancing plan suggest the company is stabilising. The stock is trading around 140p, far below its historical highs, and the market still views it as a distressed asset.

rns_narrative: The company has been actively reshaping its leadership—new director appointments, a CFO change, and a board re‑organisation—signalling an intent to reset strategic direction.
Financially, SYNT is pursuing a refinancing package and has admitted to trading, indicating liquidity pressure but also a move toward regular cash flow.
Operationally, the latest results show a modest turnaround, with the company reporting improved margins and a new contract pipeline that could lift revenue in the coming quarters.

crowd_tape.dominant_theme: Quiet optimism around a mispriced catalyst
crowd_tape.bull_narrative: Analysts such as Peel Hunt and Goldie24_7 point to a substantial upside target of 220p, arguing that the market still underestimates SYNT’s value once the company’s restructuring takes effect.
crowd_tape.bear_narrative: Concerns linger over the company’s liquidity and the risk that the refinancing deal may not materialise, which could force a further price decline or even a delisting.
crowd_tape.sentiment_shift: The chatter has moved from near‑silence to a handful of bullish posts, signalling that traders are starting to notice the mispricing but the overall mood remains cautious.

whats_happening_now.attention_check: Google Trends is flat, indicating a lack of public awareness that could trigger a surprise move when a catalyst hits.

near_term_catalysts: A positive earnings report next quarter that beats analyst expectations, especially if it shows improved gross margin and a growing contract pipeline.
Completion of the refinancing package—any announcement that the company has secured new debt or equity will lift sentiment.
A potential investment or partnership announcement from Ineos Group, which has already earmarked €200m for European chemicals firms and could bring strategic support.
Volume spikes at key support levels (around 150p) that confirm capitulation is over and a new buying wave is beginning.
expect_next.watch_for: Look for a 2‑day volume surge that exceeds 3× the 20‑day average, a break of the 150p support, and a bullish candlestick pattern on the daily chart. Also monitor any new contract signings or supply‑chain improvements that could be announced in the next 2–3 months.
expect_next.timing_regime: The stock sits in the early stages of a recovery cycle. The APEX score suggests a tight compression, so a mean‑reversion bounce is likely if the above catalysts materialise. Patience is required until the company’s cash flow stabilises.

buy_vs_sell.bull_case: SYNT’s stock is currently 70–90% below its pre‑distress levels, giving a 40% upside to the 220p target. The recent management overhaul and refinancing plan increase the probability of a turnaround. Historically, deep‑crash setups have produced large rebounds, and the current bullish chatter indicates the market is still underpricing the company.
buy_vs_sell.bear_case: Liquidity remains a risk; if the refinancing fails or the company misses key operational milestones, the price could drop further into the 100p range. A delisting scenario, while unlikely, would wipe out equity value entirely.
buy_vs_sell.risk_reward: Potential loss: 30p (down to 110p). Potential gain: 80p (up to 220p). The upside is more than twice the downside, offering a 2.7:1 risk‑reward ratio.

playbook.if_bullish: Enter at 150p after a volume spike that confirms the end of capitulation. Allocate 10–15% of the discretionary portfolio to the position, with a stop at 120p to protect against a breakout of the lower support. Use a 3‑month holding period to capture the mean‑reversion move.
playbook.if_watching: Hold cash until the stock breaks 160p on a 2‑day volume surge above 3× the 20‑day average. Alternatively, wait for a positive earnings surprise or a refinancing announcement before committing capital.
playbook.if_passing: If the company fails to secure refinancing or a key contract and the price slides below 100p with no volume support, consider exiting the position to avoid a prolonged downside.

ai_insights.bottom_line: SYNT sits at a deep crash level, poised for a mean‑reversion bounce once its refinancing and new contracts are confirmed, offering a highly asymmetric upside that outweighs the remaining downside risk.
